subjects.name: A-Level Maths
subjects.challenge: A-Level Maths moves quickly from familiar GCSE skills into abstract pure maths, mechanics, and statistics, so students often need patient support that connects each method to exam-style questions.
subjects.concepts: pure maths, mechanics, statistics, calculus, and proof

subjects.name: A-Level Further Maths
subjects.challenge: A-Level Further Maths is fast, technical, and highly cumulative, so students benefit from a tutor who can slow down difficult ideas without lowering the academic standard.
subjects.concepts: complex numbers, matrices, further calculus, mechanics, and discrete maths

subjects.name: A-Level Physics
subjects.challenge: A-Level Physics is one of the most demanding subjects on the curriculum, combining abstract theory, mathematical problem solving, practical skills, and precise exam technique.
subjects.concepts: mechanics, electric fields, quantum phenomena, waves, and nuclear physics

subjects.name: A-Level Chemistry
subjects.challenge: A-Level Chemistry can feel like three subjects at once, with students expected to link calculations, mechanisms, practical work, and detailed chemical knowledge under exam pressure.
subjects.concepts: organic chemistry, inorganic chemistry, physical chemistry, energetics, and practical analysis

subjects.name: A-Level Biology
subjects.challenge: A-Level Biology rewards both deep understanding and precise wording, so students need support that helps them connect large topics while writing answers in the language examiners expect.
subjects.concepts: cells, genetics, enzymes, physiology, ecology, and data analysis

subjects.name: A-Level Economics
subjects.challenge: A-Level Economics asks students to understand models, apply them to real contexts, and write balanced evaluation under tight time pressure.
subjects.concepts: microeconomics, macroeconomics, market failure, policy, and evaluation

subjects.name: A-Level English Literature
subjects.challenge: A-Level English Literature expects students to build fluent arguments, analyse language closely, and handle context and critical viewpoints with confidence.
subjects.concepts: close analysis, essay structure, comparison, context, and critical interpretation

subjects.name: A-Level History
subjects.challenge: A-Level History requires detailed knowledge, strong argument, and confident source or interpretation analysis, all written clearly under timed conditions.
subjects.concepts: essay planning, source analysis, interpretations, chronology, and historical argument

subjects.name: A-Level Psychology
subjects.challenge: A-Level Psychology combines studies, theories, research methods, statistics, and evaluation, so students need a clear system for organising and applying knowledge.
subjects.concepts: research methods, approaches, biopsychology, psychopathology, and evaluation

subjects.name: A-Level Geography
subjects.challenge: A-Level Geography asks students to connect physical and human processes with case studies, data, fieldwork, and extended written answers.
subjects.concepts: physical geography, human geography, case studies, fieldwork, and data interpretation

subjects.name: A-Level Computer Science
subjects.boards: AQA • OCR • Cambridge International Computer Science
subjects.challenge: A-Level Computer Science combines theory, programming, algorithms, and project work, so students need support that is both technically accurate and easy to follow.
subjects.concepts: programming, algorithms, data structures, systems architecture, and computational thinking

subjects.name: A-Level Politics
subjects.challenge: A-Level Politics requires students to understand institutions and ideas, then turn that knowledge into sharp, well-evaluated essays.
subjects.concepts: UK politics, political ideas, comparative politics, institutions, and essay evaluation
